title: Coppensia mandonii
taxonomy: Coppensia mandonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Coppensia, which is a group of plants within the larger Rubiaceae family. Coppensia mandonii is native to South America, specifically in the regions of Peru and Bolivia. The plant is known to thrive in tropical environments, often found in areas with high humidity and moderate temperatures. Coppensia mandonii's exact classification within the plant kingdom is still being studied by botanists, but it is generally classified as an angiosperm.

intro: Coppensia mandonii, a plant native to the high-altitude grasslands of Bolivia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Coppensia mandonii has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Coppensia mandonii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coppensia mandonii is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
